query: Cognition Devin Review Security Swarm automated PR scan bug detection rate comparison 2026
a_issues: Content A includes some relevant facts, but its strongest result is a third-party recap (byteiota) rather than the primary evaluation. It lacks the key Devin Review fact from the anchor that every pull request now gets a security review when opened. It also contains weak/noisy results: an unrelated general Devin review article, an uninformative X post snippet, and a Yahoo syndication fragment with little detail. As a result, an LLM would have to infer the comparison from partial and mixed-quality evidence.
b_issues: Content B also does not directly include the specific Devin Review launch wording about every PR being scanned on open; most results focus on Security Swarm. However, it includes the primary Devin evaluation page and multiple consistent corroborating sources. Some sources are syndicated press coverage rather than primary technical documentation.
reasoning: B better enables a correct answer because it contains the most directly relevant and reliable evidence for the comparison point in the query: the official Devin evaluation page states Security Swarm finds bugs in 72% of cases, highest recall tested, at roughly two-thirds the cost of the next most accurate alternative, and other results consistently restate 36/50 vulnerabilities across 14 languages and 30% lower cost per finding. It also includes the official product page saying Devin confirms vulnerabilities, writes patches, and opens PRs, plus LinkedIn content that Devin ships fixes as PRs. A has some of this, but its evidence is weaker and noisier, with the top hit being a third-party article and no direct official evaluation result. Neither set fully captures the Devin Review automated-PR-scan announcement, but B is closer overall because it has the anchor-aligned official eval source and cleaner corroboration.
